mnemonics.title: CAPACITY Steps
mnemonics.content:
C - Current Analysis
A - Anticipate Demand
P - Plan Solutions
A - Act on Plan
C - Check & Monitor
I - Improve Continuously
T - Tune as Needed
Y - Yield Results

mnemonics.title: Resource Types
mnemonics.content: HHI - Hardware, Human, Infrastructure (software is implied)

acronyms.usage: Defines performance targets like response time and uptime that capacity planning must meet
Used to evaluate cost-effectiveness of capacity planning decisions
Includes all costs - acquisition, operation, maintenance of resources
Maximum data loss acceptable, influences backup capacity planning
Maximum downtime acceptable, influences disaster recovery capacity

visualTips.visualization: Think of a fuel tank - 70-85% is the optimal fill level leaving room for refueling (unexpected demand) while not being wasteful
Vertical = stacking more books on one shelf (more power to one machine); Horizontal = adding more shelves (more machines)
Think of it as a cycle: Current Analysis → Forecast → Gap Analysis → Plan → Implement → Monitor → Repeat
Think of multiple checkout counters at a store - customers are directed to less busy counters for faster service

analogies.analogy: Like keeping a spare tire in a car - you hope not to need it, but it's essential when you do
Like buying clothes that fit - too big wastes money, too small causes discomfort
Like regular car maintenance - prevents breakdowns before they happen rather than calling tow truck after failure
Like grocery shopping - you plan meals and buy ingredients before you run out, not after
